# Discord Developer Docs: Interactions & Commands (2026)

## Summary

Official Discord documentation (updated March 2026) covering the full interactions surface. Interactions are generated by slash commands, message/user commands, message components (buttons, select menus), and modals. Apps can receive interactions via Gateway WebSocket OR HTTP POST to an Interactions Endpoint URL — the two modes are mutually exclusive. The HTTP mode requires Ed25519 signature validation and a PING/PONG handshake. Modal responses can only contain text inputs. Entry Point commands (type 4) launch Activities.

## Key quotations / statistics

- "When a user invokes a slash command, clicks a button, selects from a dropdown, or submits a modal, Discord sends your app an interaction payload that your app can respond to directly."
- "By default, your app receives interactions over the Gateway, the same persistent WebSocket connection used for all other real-time events."
- "This model [HTTP] works well for apps that only need to respond to commands and UI components. No persistent connection is required and it scales naturally with standard web infrastructure."
- "To use it [HTTP], your endpoint must validate Discord's request signatures and handle the initial PING handshake."

## Interaction types table

| Name | Value | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| PING | 1 | Gateway/HTTP handshake |
| APPLICATION_COMMAND | 2 | Slash, user, or message command |
| MESSAGE_COMPONENT | 3 | Button or select menu click |
| APPLICATION_COMMAND_AUTOCOMPLETE | 4 | Autocomplete suggestion request |
| MODAL_SUBMIT | 5 | Modal form submission |

## Callback types table (response types)

| Name | Value | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| PONG | 1 | ACK PING |
| CHANNEL_MESSAGE_WITH_SOURCE | 4 | Respond with message |
| DEFERRED_CHANNEL_MESSAGE_WITH_SOURCE | 5 | Deferred response (shows loading) |
| DEFERRED_UPDATE_MESSAGE | 6 | Components only — no loading state |
| UPDATE_MESSAGE | 7 | Components only — edit original |
| APPLICATION_COMMAND_AUTOCOMPLETE_RESULT | 8 | Autocomplete suggestions |
| MODAL | 9 | Pop-up modal |
| LAUNCH_ACTIVITY | 12 | Launch an Activity |

Note: `PREMIUM_REQUIRED` (type 10) is **deprecated**.

## Modal structure

```json
{
  "custom_id": "my_modal",
  "components": [
    {
      "type": 1,
      "components": [
        {
          "type": 4,
          "custom_id": "field_name",
          "label": "Label",
          "style": 1,
          "min_length": 1,
          "max_length": 1000,
          "placeholder": "Placeholder text",
          "required": true
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Modal `custom_id` max length: **100 characters**.

## Annotations for stinger-forge

- **Directly informs guides/00-principles.md (gateway-vs-HTTP decision tree)**: Stateless bots with no voice, presence, or member-tracking can save resources with HTTP endpoints. Stateful bots MUST use gateway.
- **guides/03-components-modals.md**: `custom_id` is max 100 chars — important for the namespacing convention (`<command>:<userId>:<action>`). Modals can only contain text inputs (no buttons/selects inside modals).
- **Critical note**: "If you are receiving Interactions over the gateway, you HAVE to respond via HTTP." Responses to gateway interactions are NOT sent back over the WebSocket.
- The docs URL pattern for the updated docs (2026) is `docs.discord.com` (not `discord.com/developers/docs`) — same content, new subdomain. Both appear valid.
